title: Combine Fit failing with large underflow in some bins

posts.name: Frederic Engelke
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I have a problem in our analysis doing cross checks for our data cards.
I am using Combine in CMSSW_11_3_4.
One example datacard is attached (data is blinded), and I am using the following commands to generate the impact plot
text2workspace.py -m 120.000000 --dataMapName=data_obs test_shape_1700.txt  -b -o  T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root -m 200 --rMin -100 --rMax 200 --robustFit 1 --doInitialFit -t -1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root  -m 200 --rMin -100 --rMax 200 --robustFit 1 --doFits -t -1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root  -m 200 -o impacts_1700.json -t -1 plotImpacts.py -i impacts_1700.json -o impacts_1700

During the fit, I get several warnings from the kind:
WARNING: underflow to -2.06524e+28 in pdf_binch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_obsOnly for bin 0, weight 5.3461
[#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(ch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935) evaluation error,
origin       : RooAbsReal::ch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935[ params=(ElectronRecoSf,ElectronTightSf,JER,JetDeepJetMediumSf,MuonMediumIsoSf,MuonMediumSf,MuonTriggerSf,PileUPWeight,PreFireWeight,QCD_estimate_unc,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_T5qqqqWW_1700_1200,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_Wjets,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_ttjets,Total_JEC,Xsec_T5qqqqWW_1700,Xsec_Wjets,Xsec_ttjets,alpha,beta,lumi,r) catParams=() ]
message      : Number of events is negative or error
server values: params=(ElectronRecoSf = 0 +/- 0.954583,ElectronTightSf = 0 +/- 0.946854,JER = 0 +/- 0.946602,JetDeepJetMediumSf = 0 +/- 0.955255,MuonMediumIsoSf = 0 +/- 0.955627,MuonMediumSf = 0 +/- 0.955334,MuonTriggerSf = 0 +/- 0.952603,PileUPWeight = 0 +/- 0.952866,PreFireWeight = 0 +/- 0.955265,QCD_estimate_unc = 0 +/- 0.987838,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_T5qqqqWW_1700_1200 = 0 +/- 1,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_Wjets = 0 +/- 0.996815,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_ttjets = 0 +/- 0.995191,Total_JEC = 0 +/- 0.463904,Xsec_T5qqqqWW_1700 = 0 +/- 1,Xsec_Wjets = 0 +/- 0.992613,Xsec_ttjets = 0 +/- 1,alpha = 1 +/- 0.05913,beta = -6.25e+27 +/- 0.0754273,lumi = 0 +/- 0.960218,r = 0 +/- 0.230451), catParams=()
FASTEXIT from pdf_binch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_obsOnly

I am not really sure how to tackle this problem of extreme underflows, maybe somebody with more experience can give me some tips on what to improve?
If I can provide any more information to tackle the problem, please ask.
Thanks in advance,
Frederic
cms_talk_test_shape_1700.pdf (105.2 KB)
cms_talk_impacts_1700.pdf (25.0 KB)
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I have a problem in our analysis doing cross checks for our data cards.
I am using Combine in CMSSW_11_3_4.

One example datacard is attached (data is blinded), and I am using the following commands to generate the impact plot

text2workspace.py -m 120.000000 --dataMapName=data_obs test_shape_1700.txt  -b -o  T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root -m 200 --rMin -100 --rMax 200 --robustFit 1 --doInitialFit -t -1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root  -m 200 --rMin -100 --rMax 200 --robustFit 1 --doFits -t -1
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d T5qqqqWW_Workspace_1700.root  -m 200 -o impacts_1700.json -t -1 plotImpacts.py -i impacts_1700.json -o impacts_1700

During the fit, I get several warnings from the kind:

WARNING: underflow to -2.06524e+28 in pdf_binch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_obsOnly for bin 0, weight 5.3461
[#0] ERROR:Eval -- RooAbsReal::logEvalError(ch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935) evaluation error, 
 origin       : RooAbsReal::ch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935[ params=(ElectronRecoSf,ElectronTightSf,JER,JetDeepJetMediumSf,MuonMediumIsoSf,MuonMediumSf,MuonTriggerSf,PileUPWeight,PreFireWeight,QCD_estimate_unc,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_T5qqqqWW_1700_1200,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_Wjets,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_ttjets,Total_JEC,Xsec_T5qqqqWW_1700,Xsec_Wjets,Xsec_ttjets,alpha,beta,lumi,r) catParams=() ]
 message      : Number of events is negative or error
 server values: params=(ElectronRecoSf = 0 +/- 0.954583,ElectronTightSf = 0 +/- 0.946854,JER = 0 +/- 0.946602,JetDeepJetMediumSf = 0 +/- 0.955255,MuonMediumIsoSf = 0 +/- 0.955627,MuonMediumSf = 0 +/- 0.955334,MuonTriggerSf = 0 +/- 0.952603,PileUPWeight = 0 +/- 0.952866,PreFireWeight = 0 +/- 0.955265,QCD_estimate_unc = 0 +/- 0.987838,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_T5qqqqWW_1700_1200 = 0 +/- 1,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_Wjets = 0 +/- 0.996815,Stat_unc_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_ttjets = 0 +/- 0.995191,Total_JEC = 0 +/- 0.463904,Xsec_T5qqqqWW_1700 = 0 +/- 1,Xsec_Wjets = 0 +/- 0.992613,Xsec_ttjets = 0 +/- 1,alpha = 1 +/- 0.05913,beta = -6.25e+27 +/- 0.0754273,lumi = 0 +/- 0.960218,r = 0 +/- 0.230451), catParams=()
FASTEXIT from pdf_binch1_DNN_Score_Node_T5qqqqWW_0.935_obsOnly

I am not really sure how to tackle this problem of extreme underflows, maybe somebody with more experience can give me some tips on what to improve?
If I can provide any more information to tackle the problem, please ask.

Thanks in advance,
Frederic

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Hi Frederic,
Maybe you solved your problem in the meantime, but in case not: the error message does point to the problem: the fit has ended up in a region where the number of events is negative. Looking at the values of the parameters I see

Frederic Engelke:

beta = -6.25e+27 +/- 0.0754273

Would it be possible to set a range for this parameter?
Not necessarily the source of the problem here, but judging by the +ve uncertainty on the signal strength in your impact plot, I think you also don’t need to let r range from -100 to 200, something like ±5 should be plenty (you could even restrict more than that).
Hope this helps,
Adinda
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Frederic,

Maybe you solved your problem in the meantime, but in case not: the error message does point to the problem: the fit has ended up in a region where the number of events is negative. Looking at the values of the parameters I see

Frederic Engelke:

beta = -6.25e+27 +/- 0.0754273

Would it be possible to set a range for this parameter?

Not necessarily the source of the problem here, but judging by the +ve uncertainty on the signal strength in your impact plot, I think you also don’t need to let r range from -100 to 200, something like ±5 should be plenty (you could even restrict more than that).

Hope this helps,
Adinda
